schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

mainEntity.name: Do Indian startups with US entities need a 409A valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if any entity in the group grants stock options or deferred compensation to US tax residents. This includes Indian companies with US flip structures, Delaware holding companies, or US subsidiaries.

mainEntity.name: How often should a 409A valuation be updated?

acceptedAnswer.text: At least every 12 months, or sooner if a material event occurs (new funding round, significant revenue milestone, M&A activity, pivot). Options granted based on an outdated 409A risk IRS penalties.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for non-compliance with Section 409A?

acceptedAnswer.text: If stock options are granted below FMV as determined under 409A, the option holder faces: (1) income inclusion in the year the option vests, (2) 20% additional tax on the deferred compensation, and (3) premium interest tax. These penalties fall on the employee, not the company.

mainEntity.name: Can one valuation satisfy both 409A and FEMA requirements?

acceptedAnswer.text: The underlying financial model can be shared, but the reports and conclusions may differ due to different regulatory requirements (FEMA requires DCF-based floor price; 409A allows multiple methods). We prepare integrated engagements that satisfy both.

Option Pricing Method Backsolve derives the fair value of common stock from a recent preferred stock financing round. It uses option pricing theory to allocate enterprise value across different equity classes (common, preferred with liquidation preferences), effectively ‘solving backward’ from the known preferred price.

mainEntity.name: Is a 409A valuation the same as an 83(b) valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. 409A determines the fair market value of common stock for option pricing. Section 83(b) relates to the election to be taxed on restricted stock at grant rather than vesting. However, both require a fair value determination, and the same valuation engagement can support both purposes if the restricted stock and options share the same underlying common stock.
